steps.title: Identify the hypotenuse
steps.content:
Angle $Q$ is a right angle, so $PR$ is the hypotenuse. Since $PQ=20$ and $PR$ is $9$ greater than $PQ$,
$$PR=20+9=29$$

steps.title: Write the area two ways
steps.content:
The area of triangle $PQR$ can be written using the legs or using the hypotenuse and altitude:
$$\frac{1}{2}(PQ)(QR)=\frac{1}{2}(PR)(QS)$$
Cancel $\frac{1}{2}$:
$$(PQ)(QR)=(PR)(QS)$$

steps.title: Find the requested ratio
steps.content: Divide both sides by $(PQ)(QS)$: $$\frac{QR}{QS}=\frac{PR}{PQ}=\frac{29}{20}$$ The answer is C.
